name: react-email
context: Build email templates with React components using @react-email/components and render them for Resend delivery
info: React Email is a library for building email templates using React components instead of raw HTML. It provides a set of pre-built, email-client-compatible components (Html, Head, Body, Container, Text, Button, Link, Img, Section, Row, Column, Hr, Preview, Heading, Tailwind) that render to cross-client HTML. Templates are authored as standard React components with props for dynamic content, then passed to Resend's `react` parameter for server-side rendering and delivery. The `@react-email/components` package bundles all components, and the `react-email` CLI provides a local dev server with hot reload for previewing templates. Agents use this skill to create professional email templates (welcome emails, invoices, notifications, marketing) without writing raw HTML tables. See [[resend/send]] for sending rendered templates and [[resend/auth]] for authentication.
triggers: create an email template with react
body:
# React Email Templates

React Email lets you build email templates using React components. Instead of writing fragile HTML tables, you compose emails from typed, email-client-compatible components.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @react-email/components react react-dom
```

For the dev preview server:

```bash
npm install react-email -D
```

## Basic Template

```tsx
import {
  Html,
  Head,
  Body,
  Container,
  Text,
  Button,
  Preview,
  Heading,
  Hr,
  Section,
} from '@react-email/components';

interface WelcomeEmailProps {
  firstName: string;
  loginUrl: string;
}

export function WelcomeEmail({ firstName, loginUrl }: WelcomeEmailProps) {
  return (
    <Html>
      <Head />
      <Preview>Welcome to Acme, {firstName}!</Preview>
      <Body style={{ backgroundColor: '#f6f9fc', fontFamily: 'sans-serif' }}>
        <Container style={{ maxWidth: '600px', margin: '0 auto', padding: '20px' }}>
          <Heading as="h1">Welcome, {firstName}!</Heading>
          <Text>Thanks for signing up for Acme. We're excited to have you.</Text>
          <Hr />
          <Section style={{ textAlign: 'center', marginTop: '20px' }}>
            <Button
              href={loginUrl}
              style={{
                backgroundColor: '#000',
                color: '#fff',
                padding: '12px 24px',
                borderRadius: '6px',
                textDecoration: 'none',
              }}
            >
              Get Started
            </Button>
          </Section>
          <Text style={{ color: '#666', fontSize: '14px', marginTop: '20px' }}>
            If you didn't create this account, please ignore this email.
          </Text>
        </Container>
      </Body>
    </Html>
  );
}
```

## Available Components

| Component | Purpose | Key Props |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| `Html` | Root `<html>` wrapper | `lang`, `dir` |
| `Head` | `<head>` with meta tags | children (styles, meta) |
| `Body` | `<body>` wrapper | `style` |
| `Container` | Centered content wrapper | `style` (maxWidth) |
| `Section` | Grouping element | `style` |
| `Row` | Table row layout | `style` |
| `Column` | Table column layout | `style`, `colSpan` |
| `Text` | Paragraph text | `style` |
| `Heading` | h1-h6 heading | `as` (`h1`-`h6`), `style` |
| `Button` | CTA button with link | `href`, `style` |
| `Link` | Anchor link | `href`, `style` |
| `Img` | Image | `src`, `alt`, `width`, `height` |
| `Hr` | Horizontal rule | `style` |
| `Preview` | Preheader text (visible in inbox) | children (text) |
| `Tailwind` | Tailwind CSS support | `config` (optional) |
| `CodeBlock` | Code display | `code`, `language`, `theme` |
| `CodeInline` | Inline code | `style` |
| `Markdown` | Render markdown | `markdownCustomStyles`, `markdownContainerStyles` |
| `Font` | Custom font face | `fontFamily`, `fallbackFontFamily`, `webFont` |

## Using with Resend

```typescript
import { Resend } from 'resend';
import { WelcomeEmail } from './emails/welcome';

const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY);

// Pass as function call, NOT JSX
const { data, error } = await resend.emails.send({
  from: 'Acme <hello@yourdomain.com>',
  to: ['user@example.com'],
  subject: 'Welcome to Acme',
  react: WelcomeEmail({ firstName: 'John', loginUrl: 'https://app.acme.com/login' }),
});
```

**Important**: Pass React components as function calls (`WelcomeEmail({ ... })`) not JSX (`<WelcomeEmail />`). The Resend SDK renders them server-side.

## Rendering to HTML String

If you need the raw HTML (for SDKs that don't support `react` parameter):

```typescript
import { render } from '@react-email/render';
import { WelcomeEmail } from './emails/welcome';

const html = await render(WelcomeEmail({ firstName: 'John', loginUrl: 'https://...' }));
const text = await render(WelcomeEmail({ firstName: 'John', loginUrl: 'https://...' }), { plainText: true });

// Use with any email provider
await resend.emails.send({
  from: 'Acme <hello@yourdomain.com>',
  to: ['user@example.com'],
  subject: 'Welcome',
  html,
  text,
});
```

## Using Tailwind CSS

```tsx
import { Tailwind, Html, Body, Container, Text, Button } from '@react-email/components';

export function StyledEmail({ name }: { name: string }) {
  return (
    <Tailwind>
      <Html>
        <Body className="bg-gray-100 font-sans">
          <Container className="max-w-xl mx-auto p-6 bg-white rounded-lg shadow">
            <Text className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Hello, {name}!</Text>
            <Text className="text-gray-600 mt-2">Welcome to our platform.</Text>
            <Button
              href="https://example.com"
              className="bg-blue-600 text-white px-6 py-3 rounded-md mt-4 inline-block"
            >
              Get Started
            </Button>
          </Container>
        </Body>
      </Html>
    </Tailwind>
  );
}
```

## Dev Preview Server

Add to `package.json`:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "email:dev": "email dev --dir ./emails --port 3030"
  }
}
```

Place templates in `./emails/` directory:

```
emails/
  welcome.tsx
  invoice.tsx
  password-reset.tsx
```

Run `npm run email:dev` to preview templates at `http://localhost:3030` with hot reload.

## Project Structure

```
src/
  emails/              # Email templates
    welcome.tsx
    invoice.tsx
    password-reset.tsx
  lib/
    send-email.ts      # Resend send helper
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| `RESEND_API_KEY` | Yes | Required when sending via Resend |

sections.title: Available components
sections.body: The `@react-email/components` package provides email-client-compatible React components: `Html`, `Head`, `Body`, `Container`, `Section`, `Row`, `Column`, `Text`, `Heading`, `Button`, `Link`, `Img`, `Hr`, `Preview`, `Tailwind`, `CodeBlock`, `CodeInline`, `Markdown`, and `Font`. These render to the HTML table layouts and inline styles required for cross-client email compatibility. Import all from `@react-email/components`.

sections.title: Integration with Resend
sections.body: Pass React Email components to Resend's `react` parameter: `resend.emails.send({ react: MyTemplate({ prop }) })`. Components must be called as functions, not JSX. The SDK renders them to HTML server-side. For non-Node.js SDKs, use `render()` from `@react-email/render` to get the HTML string, then pass it to the `html` parameter. See [[resend/send]] for full sending details.

sections.title: Tailwind CSS in emails
sections.body: Wrap templates in the `<Tailwind>` component to use Tailwind utility classes. The component inlines all Tailwind styles at render time, making them compatible with email clients. Optionally pass a `config` prop for custom theme values. This eliminates the need for manual inline styles.

sections.title: Local development preview
sections.body: Install `react-email` as a dev dependency and run `email dev --dir ./emails` to start a local preview server. Place `.tsx` templates in the specified directory. The server provides hot reload, responsive previews, and HTML source inspection. Add `"email:dev": "email dev --dir ./emails --port 3030"` to your package.json scripts.

sections.title: Rendering to HTML string
sections.body: Use `render()` from `@react-email/render` to convert React components to HTML strings: `const html = await render(MyTemplate({ props }))`. Pass `{ plainText: true }` for a text-only version. Useful when sending via SDKs that don't support the `react` parameter directly, or when you need to store/cache rendered email HTML.

links.context: Send rendered React Email templates via Resend
Tailwind CSS utility classes for email styling

related.note: React Email templates are passed to the react parameter when sending
The Tailwind component enables Tailwind CSS in email templates
React Email is commonly used in Next.js API routes for transactional emails
React Email templates are authored in TypeScript (TSX) for type-safe props

scripts.name: Create Email Template
scripts.description: Scaffold a new React Email template with common components and Resend integration

templateName.description: Name for the email template (e.g., welcome, invoice, password-reset)

props.description: Template props definition (e.g., { firstName: string, url: string })

useTailwind.description: Whether to use Tailwind CSS styling

templatePath.description: Path to the created template file

depends.reason: Templates are sent via the Resend send skill
API key is required when sending templates via Resend
